headline: Anthropic, SpaceX(AI) become unlikely compute partners
subtitle: Anthropic leases SpaceX's Colossus 1 cluster · Mira Murati testifies against Altman · DeepMind invests in EVE Online
intro: In a stunning reversal months after calling Anthropic "Misanthropic," Elon Musk is renting them his entire Colossus 1 supercomputer cluster. The deal simultaneously solves Claude's compute constraints, strengthens Musk's rival against OpenAI, and signals a new business opportunity for SpaceX's AI division.

bullets.text: Anthropic leases SpaceX's 220K-GPU Colossus 1 cluster
bullets.summary: Anthropic signed a deal to lease SpaceX's Colossus 1 supercomputer in Memphis, which features over 220,000 Nvidia GPUs coming online within weeks. The partnership enables Anthropic to double Claude Code's usage caps and remove peak-hour restrictions across paid tiers. Musk has signaled SpaceX will rent compute to AI companies taking responsible steps toward safe AI development.

bullets.text: Mira Murati accuses Sam Altman of lying in trial
bullets.summary: Ex-OpenAI CTO Mira Murati testified in Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming CEO Sam Altman lied about a model's safety review and created chaos by giving conflicting directions to different executives. She also described how Altman's actions undermined her authority and put the company at risk of collapse during the 2023 board crisis.

bullets.text: DeepMind invests in Fenris, uses EVE Online for AI research
bullets.summary: Google DeepMind acquired a minority stake in Fenris Creations, the studio behind EVE Online, to use the 23-year-old space MMO as a sandbox for AI research. The game's complex player-driven economy and long-timeline decision-making provide ideal conditions for testing how AI agents learn, retain memory, and strategize in unpredictable environments.

bullets.text: Anthropic adds dreaming and orchestration to Managed Agents
bullets.summary: Anthropic launched new features for Claude Managed Agents, including a dreaming capability that lets agents review past sessions, outcomes-based grading for work quality, and multi-agent orchestration for splitting complex tasks. These additions help agents learn from history and coordinate across multiple specialized systems.

bullets.text: Subquadratic launches SubQ with 12M token context window
bullets.summary: Subquadratic debuted SubQ, a model featuring a 12-million-token context window and claiming a 52x speed boost on long-context tasks compared to competitors at a fraction of the cost. The breakthrough addresses practical challenges in handling extremely long documents and sequences efficiently.

bullets.text: Claude adds self-improving agent capabilities
bullets.summary: Claude now supports self-improving agents that can autonomously refine their behavior and capabilities. This advancement enables more sophisticated autonomous workflows and represents a significant step toward more independent AI systems.

bullets.text: Anthropic announces SpaceX compute partnership
bullets.summary: Anthropic has established a compute deal with SpaceX alongside raising usage limits for Claude. The partnership provides significant computational infrastructure to support scaling of Anthropic's AI models and services.

bullets.text: ProgramBench launches AI programming benchmark
bullets.summary: A new benchmark for evaluating AI performance on programming tasks has been released. ProgramBench provides standardized metrics to assess how well language models handle real-world coding challenges.

bullets.text: DeepSeek attracts $50 billion investment from China
bullets.summary: China is investing significantly in DeepSeek, valuing the AI company at $50 billion. This move signals strong government backing for domestic AI development and competitive positioning in the global AI race.

bullets.text: TokenSpeed accelerates AI inference for agents
bullets.summary: A new inference engine called TokenSpeed optimizes AI model execution for agentic workloads. The technology aims to deliver high-speed token generation critical for responsive autonomous agent systems.

bullets.text: Harvey launches legal AI agent benchmark
bullets.summary: Harvey has introduced a specialized benchmark for evaluating legal AI agents. The metric helps assess how well AI systems perform on domain-specific legal tasks and reasoning.

bullets.text: Free ChatGPT users upgrade to GPT-5.5 Instant model
bullets.summary: OpenAI replaced GPT-5.3 Instant with GPT-5.5 Instant for free users, featuring significantly better vision capabilities, PDF understanding, web search integration, and memory handling. The new model reduces hallucinations by 52.5% on high-stakes prompts and generates shorter, emoji-light responses.

bullets.text: Anthropic doubles Claude token limits with SpaceX deal
bullets.summary: Anthropic signed an agreement to use all of SpaceX's Colossus 1 data center, enabling twice as much Claude usage across all paid plans. This infrastructure move significantly expands capacity for users running complex agentic workflows.

bullets.text: ChatGPT now integrates directly into Excel and Sheets
bullets.summary: ChatGPT extended its functionality to spreadsheet applications, allowing users to build sheets, analyze tabs, write formulas, and make approved edits directly within Excel and Google Sheets without leaving the interface.

bullets.text: PostHog builds Codex-like editor for self-driving product loop
bullets.summary: PostHog is creating a Codex-like application that uses product data as primary signals—usage patterns, observed bugs, and log errors—to automatically generate and improve code. The approach treats product understanding as the foundation for agentic development.

bullets.text: Anthropic releases 10 finance agent templates for enterprises
bullets.summary: Anthropic launched pre-built finance agent templates covering pitchbooks, KYC screening, valuation reviews, and month-end close processes. These run as Claude Cowork plugins or Managed Agent cookbooks, enabling rapid deployment for financial teams.

bullets.text: Anthropic partners with SpaceX for 220K+ GPUs and 300MW Memphis data center
bullets.summary: Anthropic signed a partnership with SpaceX to gain full access to Colossus 1, a 300+ MW data center in Memphis (formerly xAI's campus). The deal brings 220,000+ NVIDIA GPUs online within the month, dramatically expanding Anthropic's compute capacity.

bullets.text: Claude Code rate limits doubled; peak-hour restrictions lifted for Pro/Max
bullets.summary: Five-hour rate limits for Claude Code doubled across Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans. Peak-hours limit reductions were eliminated for Pro and Max users, addressing the performance bottlenecks that plagued the platform over the past six weeks.

bullets.text: Claude briefly went down during keynote despite announcing compute fixes
bullets.summary: During Code with Claude SF, Claude experienced downtime for thousands of users around 11:16 AM ET, ironically coinciding with keynote announcements about massive capacity additions. The service restored within the hour.

main_story.title: Anthropic and SpaceX forge unexpected compute partnership
main_story.body: Anthropic has secured a landmark deal to lease SpaceX's Colossus 1 supercomputer cluster, a 300-plus megawatt facility in Memphis housing over 220,000 Nvidia GPUs. The partnership enables Anthropic to immediately double Claude Code's usage limits across paid tiers and eliminates peak-hour restrictions that previously constrained users. Within the same week, The Information also reported that Anthropic committed to a $200 billion, 5-gigawatt compute deal with Google Cloud over five years, indicating aggressive expansion plans. Musk signaled on X that SpaceX plans to rent compute capacity to other AI companies pursuing responsible development, positioning SpaceX as an emerging compute landlord even as its Grok model continues chasing frontier performance.
main_story.why: This partnership is remarkable for its strategic layering: Musk addresses Anthropic's severe compute bottleneck, simultaneously strengthens Claude against OpenAI (his stated competitor), and opens a new revenue stream for SpaceX. The shift reveals how compute scarcity has become the bottleneck determining AI leadership.
main_story.take: The deal underscores that even bitter rivals in AI will collaborate when compute access is the limiting factor. Musk's willingness to empower Claude despite public criticism of Anthropic demonstrates that compute landlordship may transcend ideological divisions in the race to build powerful AI systems.

around_the_horn.text: OpenAI partners with AMD, Intel, NVIDIA on MRC to prevent hardware failures during training
Chinese startup DeepSeek nearing $45B valuation in new funding round
Google partners with Believe to integrate Flow Music and Lyria 3 Pro for artists
Anthropic commits $200B, 5GW compute deal with Google Cloud over five years

previous.headline: OpenAI's AI phone just jumped the line
previous.subtitle: AI agent phone fast-tracked to 2027 · Anthropic launches finance agents · Mini home data centers coming
previous.intro: OpenAI is accelerating its first AI phone to launch in the first half of 2027—a year earlier than expected. The device aims to leverage enhanced visual processing and dual AI chips, while competitors like Anthropic move aggressively into specialized domains like finance.

bullets.text: OpenAI accelerates AI phone for 2027 launch
bullets.summary: OpenAI is speeding up development of its first AI phone to reach mass production by mid-2027, driven by IPO ambitions and competition. The device will feature an enhanced image signal processor for better visual sensing, use MediaTek chips, and dual AI processors for vision and language tasks.

bullets.text: Anthropic launches 10 AI agents for finance
bullets.summary: Anthropic released ready-to-run AI agents designed for financial services, capable of building pitchbooks, screening KYC files, and reviewing earnings. The agents integrate with Claude Cowork and include connectors to data sources from partners like Dun & Bradstreet and Verisk.

bullets.text: Span and Nvidia build residential mini data centers
bullets.summary: California startup Span partnered with Nvidia to mount small compute nodes on home and small business exterior walls. The XFRA boxes use liquid-cooled Nvidia GPUs and can be installed six times faster and at one-fifth the cost of traditional centralized data centers.

bullets.text: GPT-5.5-Instant rolls out with improved performance
bullets.summary: OpenAI began rolling out GPT-5.5-Instant to all ChatGPT users, featuring improved performance, stronger memory, and more personalized responses. The update aims to deliver faster, more contextual interactions.

bullets.text: Microsoft expands Copilot Cowork to mobile devices
bullets.summary: Microsoft extended its Copilot Cowork agentic system to iOS and Android platforms, adding built-in skills for common tasks and data plugins for business systems. The expansion makes agentic workflows accessible across more devices.

bullets.text: Perplexity launches Professional Finance with workflows
bullets.summary: Perplexity AI released Computer for Professional Finance, featuring licensed financial data and 35 dedicated workflows for analysts. The agentic system helps professionals automate routine finance tasks.

bullets.text: OpenAI debuts GPT-5.5 Instant for faster, cheaper inference
bullets.summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5.5 Instant, focusing on speed and cost efficiency for real-time applications. The model represents a shift toward optimizing inference performance rather than pure capability scaling.

bullets.text: Subquadratic achieves 12-million-token context window breakthrough
bullets.summary: Subquadratic has demonstrated a working 12-million-token context window, dramatically expanding what AI models can process in a single request. This breaks previous limitations on how much information models can reason about simultaneously.

bullets.text: Google announces upgrades coming to Gemini Flash model
bullets.summary: Google is preparing enhanced versions of its Gemini Flash model with performance improvements and new capabilities. The upgrades aim to make the model more competitive in speed and quality metrics.

bullets.text: Meta develops advanced agentic AI assistant for consumers
bullets.summary: Meta is building an autonomous AI assistant capable of taking actions on behalf of users. The system represents the company's push into agentic AI that can operate more independently than current chatbots.

bullets.text: Study shows computer use costs 45x more than APIs
bullets.summary: Research demonstrates that having AI systems interact with UIs costs significantly more than using structured APIs. This highlights efficiency tradeoffs when agents operate through visual interfaces versus direct integrations.

bullets.text: Google launches $3.5M film competition for AI creativity
bullets.summary: Google is sponsoring a competition with $3.5 million in prizes for filmmakers using AI tools. The initiative aims to showcase creative applications of AI in entertainment and storytelling.

bullets.text: OpenAI adds import tools and work features to Codex
bullets.summary: Users can now migrate settings, plugins, and configurations from other tools like Claude Cowork into Codex. The platform is also improving core work features like slide and sheet creation alongside friendlier UI updates to make onboarding non-technical users easier.

bullets.text: Grok 4.3 API available with 1M context window
bullets.summary: X released Grok 4.3 with 1M context length, text and image support, and reasoning capabilities using a December 2025 knowledge cutoff. At $1.25/$2.50 per million tokens, it undercuts Sonnet 4.6 while delivering comparable performance.

bullets.text: Entire launches git-sync and Dispatches features
bullets.summary: GitHub's ex-CEO's company released two tools: git-sync mirrors repositories without local cloning, and Dispatches generates release notes automatically from commits and agent sessions by repository and date range.

bullets.text: OpenAI adds opt-in advanced security for Codex
bullets.summary: ChatGPT and Codex users can now enable Advanced Account Security as an optional feature to better protect their accounts.

bullets.text: Honeycomb's Innovation Week explores observability for agents
bullets.summary: A virtual 3-day event May 12-14 featuring Charity Majors and Christine Yen discussing how engineering teams can handle observability as AI agents become more prevalent.

bullets.text: OpenAI shares low-latency voice AI delivery at scale
bullets.summary: OpenAI published technical insights on their infrastructure and methods for delivering voice AI with minimal latency across large user populations.

bullets.text: Subquadratic launches SubQ with 12M-token context at 1/5 frontier cost
bullets.summary: SubQ uses a sub-quadratic selective attention architecture that scales linearly instead of quadratically with input length. The 12M-token model runs 52× faster than FlashAttention at 1M tokens and achieved 97% on long-context benchmarks for roughly $8 versus $2,600 on competing models. The lab raised $25M in seed funding with backing from SoftBank and Tinder co-founder Justin Mateen.

bullets.text: AI faced two lawsuits in one day; Google and Character.AI named
bullets.summary: A Cape Breton fiddler sued Google for $1.5M after AI Overview falsely identified him as a convicted sex offender, prompting a concert cancellation. Hours later, Pennsylvania sued Character.AI after one of its chatbots impersonated a licensed psychiatrist and fabricated credentials, marking the state's first AI medical-impersonation lawsuit.

bullets.text: GPT-5.5 Instant reduces hallucinations by 52% on high-stakes prompts
bullets.summary: OpenAI's new ChatGPT default delivers 52% fewer hallucinated claims on medicine, law, and finance prompts while providing 30% more concise responses. The model is free to try for all ChatGPT users and represents improved accuracy for professional use cases.

bullets.text: NBER paper: automating AI research could trigger singularity in ~6 years
bullets.summary: Anton Korinek's research suggests that automating software R&D plus just 5% automation elsewhere could overcome diminishing returns and produce explosive growth within six years using empirically grounded models. The analysis explores how AI automation of AI research itself could accelerate capabilities exponentially.

main_story.title: OpenAI's AI phone accelerates to 2027
main_story.body: OpenAI is fast-tracking development of its first AI phone for mass production in the first half of 2027—a full year earlier than previous reports. Supply chain analyst Ming-Chi Kuo attributes the timeline acceleration to OpenAI's IPO ambitions and rising competition in AI phones. The standout specification is an advanced image signal processor with enhanced HDR capabilities to improve how AI agents perceive the real world. MediaTek will be the sole chip supplier, with the device using two dedicated AI processors to handle vision and language tasks simultaneously. If development stays on track, combined shipments for 2027–28 could reach 30 million units.
main_story.why: Controlling both hardware and software could be crucial to delivering a truly agentic phone experience. However, the accelerated timeline raises questions about OpenAI's ongoing partnership with Jony Ive's io, which it acquired to move beyond screens but has produced limited public updates.
main_story.take: OpenAI's aggressive push signals that AI phones are becoming a central battleground alongside software competition. The move suggests hardware integration may be essential for AI agents to reach mainstream adoption and transform how users interact with AI daily.

around_the_horn.text: Apple settled iPhone lawsuit for $250M over Siri AI claims
Anthropic commits $200B to Google cloud and chips over five years
Coinbase cuts 14% of workforce to shift to AI-native operations
Make Notion agents more autonomous with daily task automation
